title: Palicourea nitidella
intro: Palicourea nitidella is a plant species commonly known as "Kuru-Kuru" or "Kuru", belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea nitidella include alkaloids such as akuammicine, akuammidine, and akuammidine acetate, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the form of tinctures and extracts to leverage its medicinal benefits.

This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, consisting of over 600 genera and 13,000 species.
